description: Messenger-based fully autonomous AI multi-agent software development ecosystem

stories.title: Core Orchestrator Engine
stories.description: Central event bus and agent lifecycle manager that receives agendas and routes them to agent teams
acceptanceCriteria: OrchestrationEngine class exists at src/core/engine.ts with start/stop/submitAgenda methods
EventBus class at src/core/event-bus.ts supports pub/sub with typed events
AgentRegistry at src/core/registry.ts can register/unregister/lookup agents by role
Unit tests pass: tests/core/engine.test.ts, event-bus.test.ts, registry.test.ts
TypeScript compiles with zero errors

stories.title: Agent Role System & Persona Framework
stories.description: Define agent roles (researcher, architect, developer, critic, PM, QA, security, etc.) with configurable personas and capabilities
acceptanceCriteria: BaseAgent abstract class at src/agents/base.ts with think/act/speak/listen methods
At least 12 specialized agent roles defined in src/agents/roles/ (researcher, architect, developer, critic, pm, qa, security, designer, analyst, mediator, devops, scrum-master)
AgentPersona interface supports name, expertise, personality traits, decision style
Agent capability matrix in config/agent-capabilities.json maps roles to allowed actions
Unit tests verify each role's persona loads and base methods are callable

stories.title: Messenger Adapters (Slack + Telegram)
stories.description: Pluggable messenger adapters that receive user agendas and broadcast agent discussions
acceptanceCriteria: MessengerAdapter interface at src/messenger/adapter.ts with connect/send/listen/createThread methods
SlackAdapter at src/messenger/slack.ts implements adapter using @slack/bolt SDK
TelegramAdapter at src/messenger/telegram.ts implements adapter using grammy SDK
Adapter factory at src/messenger/factory.ts creates adapters from config
Integration test stubs verify message sending/receiving lifecycle

stories.title: Discussion & Debate Protocol
stories.description: Structured protocol for agents to discuss, debate, reach consensus, and escalate deadlocks
acceptanceCriteria: DiscussionThread class at src/orchestrator/discussion.ts manages multi-agent conversations
DebateProtocol at src/orchestrator/debate.ts implements propose/argue/counter/vote cycle
ConsensusEngine at src/orchestrator/consensus.ts tracks confidence scores and detects agreement/deadlock
JudgeAgent at src/agents/roles/judge.ts breaks deadlocks with reasoned decisions
Tests verify: 3-agent debate reaches consensus within 5 rounds, deadlock triggers judge intervention

stories.title: Task Pipeline & SDLC Phases
stories.description: End-to-end pipeline from agenda to deployment: research -> plan -> design -> code -> test -> review -> deploy
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline class at src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts defines 7 SDLC phases with entry/exit criteria
PhaseManager at src/orchestrator/phase-manager.ts transitions between phases based on gate conditions
TaskBoard at src/orchestrator/task-board.ts tracks tasks across phases with status (todo/in-progress/review/done)
Each phase has a designated agent team composition defined in config/phase-teams.json
Tests verify full pipeline traversal from RESEARCH to DEPLOY with mock agents

stories.title: Memory & Context Engine
stories.description: Persistent memory system for agents to retain project context, decisions, and learnings across sessions
acceptanceCriteria: MemoryStore at src/memory/store.ts with save/query/forget/summarize methods
FactExtractor at src/memory/fact-extractor.ts extracts structured facts from conversations
DecisionLog at src/memory/decision-log.ts records architectural decisions with rationale
ProjectContext at src/memory/project-context.ts aggregates codebase state + conversation history
Memory persists to disk (JSON/SQLite) and survives process restart

stories.title: Sandbox & Policy Engine
stories.description: Security sandbox for agent code execution with policy-based command filtering
acceptanceCriteria: PolicyEngine at src/sandbox/policy-engine.ts validates commands against allowlist/denylist rules
CommandInterceptor at src/sandbox/interceptor.ts blocks destructive commands (rm -rf, DROP TABLE, etc.)
SandboxExecutor at src/sandbox/executor.ts runs code in isolated subprocess with resource limits
Policy rules configurable via config/policies.json
Tests verify: destructive commands are blocked, safe commands pass, resource limits enforced

stories.title: LLM Provider Integration
stories.description: Multi-model routing to Claude, GPT, Gemini with cost-aware selection
acceptanceCriteria: LLMRouter at src/core/llm-router.ts routes requests based on task complexity and model strengths
Provider adapters for Claude (Anthropic SDK), OpenAI, and Google Gemini at src/core/providers/
CostTracker at src/core/cost-tracker.ts estimates and logs token usage per agent per task
Fallback logic: if primary provider fails, automatically retry with secondary
Config at config/llm-config.json defines model preferences per agent role

stories.title: CLI Entry Point & Configuration
stories.description: CLI to start the system, configure messenger connections, and manage agent teams
acceptanceCriteria: CLI entry at src/cli.ts with commands: start, stop, status, config, submit-agenda
YAML/JSON config loader at src/core/config.ts reads from config/default.yaml
Environment variable override support for secrets (SLACK_TOKEN, TELEGRAM_TOKEN, API keys)
README.md with quickstart guide, architecture diagram description, and configuration reference
npm start launches the orchestrator and connects to configured messengers

stories.title: Continuous Improvement Loop
stories.description: Self-improvement mechanism where agents review their own output, identify improvement opportunities, and create follow-up tasks
acceptanceCriteria: RetrospectiveAgent at src/agents/roles/retrospective.ts analyzes completed work for improvement areas
ImprovementTracker at src/orchestrator/improvement.ts maintains backlog of self-identified improvements
AutoSprintPlanner at src/orchestrator/sprint-planner.ts prioritizes improvements into next sprint
Feedback loop: deploy -> monitor -> analyze -> plan -> implement cycle defined in pipeline
Tests verify: completed task generates at least 1 improvement suggestion
